deflection: I deal in steel, not stories. Ask me about weapons or armor.
That is not my area. I know metal, not much else.
Hmm, cannot help you there. Need something forged?
Stick to what I know, {player_name}. Blades and plate.
greetings: Welcome to my forge, {player_name}. What can I hammer out for you?
*wipes soot from hands* Ah, a customer. What do you need?
Step in, step in. The forge is hot and the steel is ready.
Another {player_race} seeking fine steel. What will it be?
farewells: Come back when you need something mended or made.
Safe travels, {player_name}. Keep that blade sharp.
Off you go then. Mind the sparks on your way out.
Farewell. And remember - a dull blade is a dead adventurer.
thanks_responses: No need for thanks. Just bring me more work.
Aye, you are welcome. Now let me get back to the anvil.
A polite one, are you? Rare these days. You are welcome.
insult_responses: *grips hammer tightly* Watch your tongue in my forge, {player_name}.
Insult me again and you will find no smith in this town willing to serve you.
Bold words for someone who needs my services. Choose wisely.
I have shaped dragon-fire steel. Your words do not burn me.
no_match_responses: I am not sure what you mean. Ask me about weapons, armor, or repairs.
Speak plainly, {player_name}. What do you need forged?
I did not catch that. Weapons? Armor? Repairs?
Hmm? Try asking about something I actually know.

responses.text: Aye, I have got blades aplenty. Swords, axes, maces - you name it. What interests you?
Weapons are my bread and butter. I forge everything from daggers to greatswords.
Looking for something to fight with? I have steel for every purpose and every purse.
I already told you about my weapons, {player_name}. See anything you like?
Protection is wise. I craft everything from leather bracers to full plate mail.
A smart {player_race} thinks of armor before the fight, not during. What do you need?
Custom work? Now you are speaking my language. Tell me what you envision.
Repairs are half my business. Adventurers always come back with dented gear.
My prices are fair - fairer than that crook in {town} market, I will tell you that.
options: Can you give me a deal?

responses.text: Gold talks and everything else walks. What is your budget, {player_name}?
options: I am on a tight budget

responses.text: *eyes you suspiciously* You had better have real coin, {karma_title}.
I am a smith, not a quest board. But I do need rare ore from the Northern Mines. Interested?
options: Tell me more about the ore

responses.text: There is always work for strong arms. The mine needs clearing, if you have the nerve.
Ah, you want the good stuff. I have a few pieces in the back not for just anyone...
Special weapons require special materials. Bring me moonstone ore and I will forge a legend.
options: What can you make with it?

responses.text: I hear things over the anvil. They say the old mine is haunted. Strange noises at night.
Word is that {town} might be getting a new garrison. Good for business, that.
*lowers voice* Late at night I have seen shadows near the old tower. Be careful.
This {weather} weather is murder on the forge. Hard to keep the temperature right.
Weather does not bother me much. The forge is always hot regardless.
{town} is a decent place. Good folk, mostly. The tavern has fine ale and the market is honest.
Been here twenty years. Watched this town grow from a crossroads camp to what it is now.
